vocab.meaning: chờ đợi, canh chừng (dịp giảm giá hoặc mẫu mới)
vocab.example: I always look out for seasonal sales to grab branded sneakers at a more reasonable price.

vocab.meaning: đảm bảo, khẳng định chất lượng của cái gì
vocab.example: I can certainly vouch for the durability of Nike shoes, as my last pair lasted for over three years.

vocab.meaning: lừa đảo, bán giá quá đắt (thường dùng để nói về hàng giả hoặc giá thương hiệu quá cao)
vocab.example: I feel like some high-end luxury brands really rip you off by charging ten times the actual production cost.

vocab.meaning: tiết kiệm tiền (để mua một món đồ cụ thể)
vocab.example: It took me three months to save up for these limited-edition Jordans, but I don't regret it.

vocab.meaning: tung ra thị trường, giới thiệu sản phẩm mới
vocab.example: Whenever high-street brands bring out a new collection, there are usually long queues outside the malls.
With so many famous brands available, it’s often hard to narrow down my choice to just one pair.
I find it hard to do away with my old Adidas because they are still so incredibly comfortable.
I’ve been keeping an eye on the latest Yeezy drop, hoping to secure a pair before they sell out.

vocab.meaning: trả một cái giá cắt cổ
vocab.example: If you want the latest designer collaborations, you often have to pay through the nose.

vocab.meaning: chọn mua, ưa chuộng cái gì
vocab.example: When I need professional footwear, I usually go for reputable brands known for their craftsmanship.
